title: bistorta emodi subsp. dependens
intro: Bistorta emodi subsp. dependens, also known as Himalayan bistort, is a plant species that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Bistorta emodi subsp. dependens include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
